name: The Earliest Moment When Everyone Become Friends
title: The Earliest Moment When Everyone Become Friends

discussion_posts.name: YuYu_online
discussion_posts.content: the problem does not clarify clear if the timestamp is in increasing order

discussion_posts.name: etanila
discussion_posts.content: The example explanation is a huge hint on Union Find.

discussion_posts.name: baxterbarlow
discussion_posts.content: Heads up, the logs are not sorted so make sure to do that first (but the author should have definitely specified that)

discussion_posts.name: nllsdfx
discussion_posts.content: Explain me pls, why my answer (8) is wrong when logs = [[9,3,0],[0,2,1],[8,0,1],[1,3,2],[2,2,0],[3,3,1]], n = 4?\n\n3-0, 2-1, 0-1? 0 connected with 1 and 3, and 1 knows about 2, right? Is it a bug?

discussion_posts.name: parkr96
discussion_posts.content: Took me an embarrassing amount of time to realize logs isn\'t sorted by timestamp from the start :D lol

discussion_posts.name: amt93
discussion_posts.content: I should have read the examples closer before starting. This question confused me by using acquaintances and friends interchangeably. I was thinking the acquaintance relation would be a separation of 1 instead of it all being one group. So I may gain someone\'s friends as my own acquaintances, but I won\'t gain their acquaintances. That\'s on me, but better to just use one word imo

discussion_posts.name: jasperjoe
discussion_posts.content: I am not quite familiar with Union-Find. Hopefully someone could share other ways to solve this kind of problems.

discussion_posts.name: navaneeth98
discussion_posts.content: Leetcode explore cards on graphs are really great man. I have been doing them and came across this question and was able to solve this in less than 10 mins. From being a noob at graphs to be able to do this, it feels really nice.

discussion_posts.name: anujamin01
discussion_posts.content: The interchangeable use of "acquaintance" and "friend" makes the explanation of this problem way more difficult to understand than it should be.

discussion_posts.name: DivSar_15
discussion_posts.content: This problem is similar to [Redundant connection](https://leetcode.com/problems/redundant-connection/). We\'re required to get the timestamp of the last non-redundant connecting edge between two disjoint sets.
